etymology.description: semantic 足(足) (zú, "foot") + phonetic 夸(夸) (kuā, "boast") -> straddle
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 足(足) (zú, "foot") represents the physical action of stepping or spreading one's legs. The character 夸(夸) (kuā, "boast") contributes the pronunciation but not the meaning. Together, they convey the idea of spreading one's feet apart, capturing the sense of 'straddling'.

examples.chinese: 他跨过了小溪(他跨過了小溪)。
这座桥跨越了两座山谷(這座橋跨越了兩座山谷)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zuò qiáo kuàyuè le liǎng zuò shāngǔ.

examples.chinese: 他跨上了马(他跨上了馬)，开始了旅程(開始了旅程)。
examples.pinyin: Tā kuàshàng le mǎ, kāishǐ le lǚchéng.
examples.english: He mounted the horse and began his journey.
